title: Karaage Crispy Japanese Fried Chicken

seo.title: Karaage Recipe: Crispy Japanese Fried Chicken
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make the best crispy Karaage at home with this authentic Japanese fried chicken recipe. Get pro tips for juicy, tender chicken every time!
seo.h1: Karaage Recipe: Crispy Japanese Fried Chicken
h2Headings: How to Make Karaage: Step-by-Step Instructions
Pro Tips for the Best Karaage
Serving Suggestions for Japanese Fried Chicken
seo.imageAlt: Plate of crispy Karaage, authentic Japanese fried chicken, garnished with lemon and parsley.
seo.imageCaption: Achieve perfectly crispy and juicy Karaage, just like your favorite Japanese restaurant, with our easy-to-follow recipe!

description: Karaage is the quintessential Japanese fried chicken, renowned for its incredibly crispy exterior and wonderfully juicy, flavorful interior. Each bite offers a delightful crunch followed by tender, marinated chicken bursting with umami. Perfect as an appetizer, side dish, or main course, it is a beloved comfort food across Japan.

instructions.title: Prepare the Chicken
instructions.text: Cut chicken thighs into bite-sized pieces, about 3-4 cm. Pat them very dry with paper towels to ensure a crispy result. This removes excess moisture.

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine soy sauce, sake, mirin, grated ginger, grated garlic, and sesame oil. Add the chicken pieces and mix well to coat. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 1-2 hours, to allow flavors to penetrate.

instructions.title: Prepare the Coating
instructions.text: In a separate bowl, whisk the egg. Add the marinated chicken to the egg and mix until well coated. In another shallow dish, combine potato starch and all-purpose flour.

instructions.title: Coat the Chicken
instructions.text: Take each chicken piece and dredge it thoroughly in the potato starch and flour mixture, ensuring it is completely covered. Shake off any excess coating. Repeat for all chicken pieces.

instructions.title: First Fry (Low Temperature)
instructions.text: Heat frying oil in a deep pot or Dutch oven to 160C (320F). Carefully add chicken pieces in batches, making sure not to overcrowd the pot. Fry for 3-4 minutes until lightly golden and cooked through. Remove and drain on a wire rack.

instructions.title: Second Fry (High Temperature)
instructions.text: Increase the oil temperature to 180C (350F). Return the chicken to the hot oil in batches and fry for another 1-2 minutes until deeply golden brown and extra crispy. This double-frying technique is key for ultimate crispness.

instructions.title: Serve Immediately
instructions.text: Remove the crispy Karaage from the oil and drain on a wire rack lined with paper towels to absorb excess oil. Serve immediately with lemon wedges and a side of Japanese mayonnaise.

tips: For maximum crispiness, always double-fry your Karaage. The first fry cooks the chicken through, and the second fry at a higher temperature creates that irresistible crunch.
Potato starch (katakuriko) is crucial for authentic Karaage crispiness. Cornstarch can be a substitute, but potato starch gives a lighter, more delicate crunch.
Serve Karaage hot and fresh. It is best enjoyed immediately after frying. For leftovers, reheat in an air fryer or oven to regain some crispness.
article:
Japanese cuisine is beloved for its delicate flavors and masterful techniques. While sushi and ramen often take center stage, Karaage, the incredibly crispy and juicy Japanese fried chicken, is a culinary gem equally deserving of adoration. A staple in izakayas, bento boxes, and home kitchens across Japan, its global popularity has soared. If you're ready to elevate your fried chicken game, uncovering the secrets behind authentic Japanese fried chicken is your delicious first step. Prepare to dive into one of Japan's most cherished comfort foods, a dish promising an explosion of flavor and texture with every bite.

## What is Karaage?
Karaage (pronounced kah-rah-ah-geh) literally translates to "Tang-fried," a cooking method perfected in Japan. It consists of bite-sized chicken pieces, typically thigh, marinated in a flavorful blend of soy sauce, sake, mirin, ginger, and garlic. These marinated pieces are then lightly coated in a starch (usually potato starch) and deep-fried until golden brown and irresistibly crispy. Unlike many Western fried chickens, this Japanese delicacy boasts a thin, delicate, and incredibly crunchy crust that shatters to reveal tender, juicy meat. It offers a perfect balance of savory, aromatic, and umami flavors, making it a universally loved and truly addictive dish.

## Ingredients for Crispy Karaage
Crafting the perfect Karaage begins with selecting quality ingredients.

For the Chicken:
-   **Boneless, Skin-on Chicken Thighs:** Essential for juicy, flavorful results. Cut into 1.5 to 2-inch
